families.evidence: date/version naming and same .NET WASM lineage
families.decision: protect both until commit/tree diff identifies canonical lineage and unique work

families.evidence: shared CPD/helpdesk/event domain; one member is zero-size and one is private
families.decision: do not infer supersession from date; inspect business modules, contents and ancestry

families.evidence: shared .NET/Blazor technology with mixed sample and application purposes
families.decision: technology cluster only; split into templates, experiments and business apps before duplicate decisions

families.evidence: overlapping AI/agent domain with probable external origins
families.decision: compare origin, license and unique commits before migration

families.evidence: both address persistent agent memory/context but may use different abstractions
families.decision: compare data model, learning loop, retrieval lifecycle and integration value; not yet duplicates
catalog provenance, licenses and update cadence; merge only normalized original material

families.evidence: tool execution and workflow/social automation relationship
families.decision: treat as integration candidates, not duplicates; evaluate trust boundaries and connector reuse
benchmark architecture and operational fit; preserve upstream boundaries
keep separate unless profile repository embeds redundant generated copies

families.evidence: design/visual communication overlap but materially different roles
families.decision: keep separate; inspect whether open-design vendors or embeds Mermaid assets

warning: Name similarity and domain overlap are discovery signals only. Canonical-version decisions require branch, commit ancestry, manifest, license, upstream and unique-file comparison.
